steps.title: Identify the evidence Brierley uses against Wright
steps.content: The KEY SIGNAL is Brierley's revised timeline: the humid period ended 500 years earlier than researchers previously calculated. This timing is decisive—if the drying started before Neolithic pastoralism could have caused it, then Wright's causal claim doesn't hold.

steps.title: Apply the timing and eliminate the distractors
steps.content:
Brierley's argument is that the timing proves pastoralism couldn't have caused desertification—the drying happened before Neolithic peoples could significantly affect it.
  • Choice A — 'revised timeline... mode of subsistence likely didn't cause desertification': matches Brierley's timing-based conclusion exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'pastoralism was only one of many behaviors...': misreads Brierley, who argues pastoralism did NOT cause changes, not that it was one of several causes. ✗
  • Choice C — 'pastoralism can have both beneficial and deleterious effects': misreads Brierley, who argues pastoralism preserved vegetation, not that it had mixed effects. ✗
  • Choice D — 'more research needs to be conducted': misreads Brierley, who makes a definitive claim from his model results rather than calling for more research. ✗
